items.title: Del Toro and cast bring 'Frankenstein' to Venice
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Guillermo del Toro, Mia Goth and Oscar Isaac walked the red carpet in Venice for the premiere of their latest film, "Frankenstein." (Aug. 30)

items.title: EU meet in Denmark to discuss Israel and Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Some ministers stressed the need for a consistent message and pushed for concrete measures against Israel, which has begun ground operations in Gaza City.

items.title: Jacob Elordi says the monsters of the world are "the men in suits"
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Jacob Elordi may play Frankenstein's monster in Guillermo del Toro's new adaptation of the Mary Shelly classic, but at the movie's Venice Film Festival press conference Saturday, the actor said the real monsters of the world are "the men in suits." (Aug. 30)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Thousands of redheads from all over the world gather in the Netherlands
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The 2025 edition of the Redhead Days festival includes music, food trucks and workshops tailored to particular needs of redheads - including make up explainers and skin cancer prevention. Organizers expect over several thousand attendees from some 80 countries to join during the three-day event.

(AP video by Aleksandar Furtula)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Guillermo Del Toro isn’t afraid of AI
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Guillermo Del Toro says he isn’t afraid of AI but does fear “natural stupidity." The filmmaker made the comments at a Venice Film Festival press conference Saturday for his new movie, "Frankenstein." (Aug. 30)

items.title: Chinese President Xi Jinping meets world leaders in Tianjin as China hosts SCO Summit
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Chinese President Xi Jinping on Saturday met with visiting heads of state and leaders of international organizations attending the latest Shanghai Cooperation Organization summit in the port city of Tianjin.

items.title: Minneapolis Mayor pushes for more than prayer after school shooting
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>American opinion makers were heated once again this week in a debate over the role of prayer in the wake of a mass shooting at a Minneapolis Catholic school after the city’s mayor urged that the situation was beyond thoughts and prayers. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Plumes of smoke over Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Several plumes of smoke were seen rising over the Gaza skyline on Saturday as Israel said it will slow or halt humanitarian aid into parts of northern Gaza.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Dozens of Palestinians line up to get rice from a charity kitchen in central Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Earlier this month the leading authority on food crises said that Gaza City was in famine and that half a million people across the strip were facing catastrophic levels of hunger. On Saturday the health ministry in Gaza said 10 people died as a result of starvation and malnutrition over the past 24 hours, among them three children.

(AP production by Wafaa Shurafa)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites to low-Earth orbit
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>It was the 15th flight for the first stage booster supporting this mission. The first stage landed on a 'droneship', a barge used by SpaceX, that is stationed in the Pacific Ocean.

items.title: Taking a dip Labor Day weekend? Swimmers face fecal contamination at beaches along US coastline
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of Americans will head to beaches for one last summer splash this Labor Day weekend, and many of them will arrive at beaches where swimming is not advised because of unsafe levels of fecal contamination. (AP video: Mingson Lau)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Firefighters battle flames in Zaporizhzhia as emergency service says Russian strikes hit area
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Eight people were hospitalized, including three children, they said, while fires were put out in residential apartment buildings, houses a service station and a cafe. The latest attack comes amid a renewed push to bring the three-year old war to an end. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Three Scottish brothers set a world record for fastest Pacific row
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Jamie, Ewan and Lachlan Maclean completed the journey in 139 days, arriving in Cairns, Australia. They rowed over 9,000 miles non-stop from Peru, becoming the first team to achieve the full crossing from South America to Australia.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​
